mnemonics: CPU, RAM, and Disk, tell the story of your system's risk.
keyPoints: Baseline performance under normal load to understand 'healthy' system behavior.
Correlate metrics (e.g., high CPU with high disk I/O) to identify the root cause of issues.
Forecast future capacity needs by analyzing usage trends over time.
commonMistakes: Focusing on individual metrics in isolation instead of the story they tell together.
Waiting for a performance crisis to occur before starting to monitor and plan.
Confusing maximum capacity with usable capacity, ignoring the need for performance headroom.
